prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on a nearly black frame, with no clearly visible figures or objects.

It then cuts to a black-and-white interior scene resembling a study or office. A middle-aged man in a suit and tie sits in an upholstered chair. Behind him are bookshelves, a fireplace or mantel area, framed wall decor, and table lamps. He appears to be looking down at something in his hands or on his lap, with a small table nearby.

The next shot is a close-up of a small bottle held between fingers. The label is centered and readable in part: the large brand name appears as “Flavettes.” Below it, the label includes the words “helps stop SMOKING and APPETITE,” though some smaller text near the bottom is not fully clear in the frame. The bottle has a screw cap and appears to contain small items, though the exact contents are not clearly visible.

The commercial then returns to the seated man in the study setting. He holds the same small bottle up beside his face, presenting it toward the camera. His posture and gaze are directed forward, as if addressing the viewer. The bookshelves and furniture remain visible in the background.

In the final sampled frame, the man is shown closer to the camera, still seated. He holds the bottle upright near his face with one hand and points toward it with the other hand, emphasizing the product visually. The product label faces the viewer, though at this distance only the larger parts of the label are partly legible. The scene remains in the same office/library environment.
